notes: AAOC C321 Control Systems, BITS Pilani (Rajasthan), Compre dated 08-12-2007. Max Marks 120. Part A (30 marks): 22 fill-in-the-blank questions, 30 blanks at 1 mark each. Part B (90 marks): 5 long-answer questions. Older course code AAOC C321 corresponds to current EEE/INSTR/ECE F242. All stems reconstructed from solution layout.

questions.stem: Lumped parameters are characterized by _______ (differential eqs. / partial differential eqs.)
common_errors: Confusing lumped with distributed-parameter systems which use PDEs
questions.solution_method: Lumped parameter systems use ordinary differential equations; distributed parameter systems use partial differential equations.

questions.stem: System described by equation Y = d²x/dt² + dx/dt + 7√x, where X is input and Y is output, is a _______ system (Non linear/linear and Time variant/invariant).
common_errors: Missing that √x makes it nonlinear
Incorrectly classifying as time-variant
questions.solution_method: The √x term makes the system nonlinear. Coefficients have no explicit time dependence so it is time-invariant. Answer: Non-linear, Time Invariant (NLTI).

questions.stem: In an ideal position control servo mechanism, back emf constant is numerically equal to _______ constant.
common_errors: Confusing with armature resistance or gain constant
questions.solution_method: In an ideal DC servomotor, Kb = Kt (back-emf constant equals torque constant) in consistent SI units.

questions.stem: For a unity negative feedback system, forward path gain is K/(s+9). Sensitivity of the system in case of open loop and closed loop to small changes in K (K=0.4) at ω=1 rad/s is _______ and _______ respectively.
common_errors: Open-loop sensitivity is always 1, not the magnitude of T
Forgetting to evaluate at specified K and ω
questions.solution_method: Open-loop sensitivity S=1. Closed-loop sensitivity S_CL = 1/(1+GH). With G=K/(s+9), H=1, K=0.4, s=jω=j: S_CL = (s+9)/(s+9+K) at s=j1 → |S_CL| ≈ 0.958. Answer: 1 and 0.958.

questions.stem: In Q.4, if required time constant for closed loop system is 10 ms, find the value of K and corresponding steady-state gain.
common_errors: Equating open-loop pole to required time constant instead of closed-loop pole
questions.solution_method: CL TF = K/(s+9+K). CL pole at -(9+K). Time constant τ = 1/(9+K) = 10ms → 9+K=100 → K=91. Steady-state gain = K/(9+K) = 91/100 = 0.91.

questions.stem: If a first order system works in open loop mode, its steady state gain and speed of response is _______ and _______ respectively, as compared to closed loop mode.
common_errors: Thinking open-loop is faster because there's no feedback delay
questions.solution_method: Open-loop has higher DC gain but slower response (larger time constant) compared to closed-loop, which trades gain for bandwidth. Answer: high gain, low speed of response.

questions.stem: A 6-stack stepper motor has _______ numbers of teeth if the angular displacement between stacks of stator teeth is 4° (assuming stack rotor teeth aligns with its stator).
common_errors: Using step angle = 360/(stacks×teeth) without accounting for offset formula
questions.solution_method: Step angle = 4°, number of stacks = 6. Teeth per stack = 360/(stacks × step angle) = 360/(6×4) = 15.

questions.stem: In Synchro transmitter, at some position of its rotor the voltage in one coil is maximum while across other two is zero. This position is known as _______ and the same name is given to the control transformer rotor position if the rotors of synchro pair are at _______.
common_errors: Confusing electrical zero with mechanical zero
questions.solution_method: The position where one coil voltage is maximum and the other two are zero is called 'Electrical Zero'. For a synchro pair, the control transformer rotor is at electrical zero when rotors are at 90° to each other.

questions.stem: The Hydraulic actuator will work as an ideal integrator if leakage and _______ flow are negligible. (compressible/turbulent)
common_errors: Selecting turbulent instead of compressible
questions.solution_method: Hydraulic actuator behaves as ideal integrator when leakage and compressible flow (fluid compressibility) are negligible.

questions.stem: For the same horse power, hydraulic actuators are _______ than electrical motors. (lighter/heavier)
questions.solution_method: Hydraulic actuators have higher power-to-weight ratio than electrical motors, so they are lighter for the same horsepower.

questions.stem: Out of Pneumatic and hydraulic systems, which one has shorter response time?
common_errors: Selecting pneumatic (compressibility slows response)
questions.solution_method: Hydraulic systems have shorter response time due to near-incompressibility of hydraulic fluid compared to pneumatic systems.

questions.stem: The _______ damped step response of a second order system oscillates with constant frequency and magnitude.
common_errors: Confusing underdamped (decaying oscillation) with undamped (constant oscillation)
questions.solution_method: Undamped (ζ=0) second-order step response oscillates with constant frequency ωn and constant magnitude. Answer: undamped.

questions.stem: The response of a system for step input of 4 units is (1−e^{−4t})u(t). If this system is excited by an input of e^{−5t}u(t), find the steady-state value of the response.
common_errors: Not dividing by step amplitude to get TF
Applying Final Value Theorem without checking ROC
questions.solution_method: Step response = (1−e^{−4t})u(t) for 4u(t) input → Y(s)/X(s) = G(s) = 4/(s(s+4)) × s/4 = 1/(s+4). Input X(s) = 1/(s+5). Y(s) = 1/((s+4)(s+5)). FVT: lim s→0 of sY(s) = 0. But since input decays to zero, output SS = 0.05 or 0 (poles in LHP, input not persistent). Solution notes: 0.05 or 0 unit.
key_formulas: Final Value Theorem: lim_{t→∞} y(t) = lim_{s→0} sY(s)

questions.stem: The addition of only a zero in the closed loop transfer function results in _______ rise time and _______ peak overshoot.
common_errors: Thinking zero reduces both tr and Mp
questions.solution_method: Adding a zero to the CLTF reduces rise time and increases peak overshoot (faster but more oscillatory response).

questions.stem: For the system shown in Fig Q.15 [unity feedback with forward path G(s)=10/(s(5s+1)) and inner feedback H(s)=0.5/(s(0.25s+1))], find the position error coefficient Kp and acceleration error coefficient Ka.
common_errors: Not reducing the inner loop before computing error coefficients
Confusing system type after reduction
questions.solution_method: Reduce inner loop first, then determine type number of reduced open-loop TF for outer loop. Solution gives Kp = ∞ (type ≥ 1) and Ka = 5.

questions.stem: The open loop transfer function of a negative feedback system is K/[(s+1)(s+3)]. The range of K for which the system exhibits overdamped response is _______.
common_errors: Not finding the breakaway point on the real axis correctly
questions.solution_method: CL char. eq.: s²+4s+3+K=0. Critical (critically damped) when discriminant=0: 16−4(3+K)=0 → K=1. For overdamped: 0 < K < 1.

questions.stem: The characteristic equation of a negative feedback system is s³ + 4s² + 5s + K = 0. The range of K for the system to be stable is _______.
common_errors: Forgetting K>0 condition from the first column
questions.solution_method: Routh array: row1=[1,5], row2=[4,K], row3=[(20−K)/4, 0], row4=[K,0]. Conditions: (20−K)/4 > 0 → K<20 and K>0. Range: 0 < K < 20.

questions.stem: For a system to be stable, the gain at phase cross-over frequency should be less than _______ dB.
common_errors: Stating gain must be negative dB — correct, but answer is 0 dB (zero dB)
questions.solution_method: Gain margin is positive when |GH| < 1 (i.e., < 0 dB) at phase crossover frequency. For stability: gain < 0 dB. Answer: Zero (0 dB).

questions.stem: The transfer function of a compensation network is (s+5)/(s+0.5), this represents a _______ network. (lead/lag)
common_errors: Thinking zero > pole always means lead — need to check if zero/pole are both in LHP and zero < pole for lag
questions.solution_method: For a lag compensator, |pole| > |zero|. Here zero at −5, pole at −0.5, so |pole| < |zero|. Wait — zero=5, pole=0.5 in magnitude, zero is farther from origin than pole → this is a phase-lag network. Answer: lag.
key_formulas: Lag: zero closer to origin than pole (|z| < |p|)

questions.stem: The maximum phase lead required from a lead network is 30°. Find the value of α (or a).
common_errors: Using sin formula incorrectly

questions.stem: The frequency plot of a system is given in Fig Q.21 [polar/Nyquist plot showing gain crossover at −0.4 on real axis, phase crossover point at −1, with 110° angle]. Find the gain margin (in dB) and phase margin.
common_errors: Reading GM as |GH| at phase crossover instead of its reciprocal in dB
Measuring PM from wrong axis
questions.solution_method: From polar plot: phase crossover at −1 on real axis, gain crossover point at −0.4. GM = 1/|GH(jω_pc)| = 1/1 = 1 → but with -0.4 as gain crossover, |GH|=0.4 at gain crossover implied. Solution gives GM ≈ 7.96 ≈ 8 dB and PM = 70°.

questions.stem: In a compensation network, the zero location is at −0.5 and at dc frequency the network provides an attenuation of 14 dB. Find the location of the compensatory pole and the frequency at which it provides maximum phase lead.
common_errors: Confusing attenuation at DC with gain instead of attenuation factor
Wrong formula for max phase frequency
questions.solution_method: DC attenuation = 20 log(z/p) = −14 dB → z/p = 10^(−14/20) ≈ 0.2. Zero at 0.5 → pole = 0.5/0.2 = 2.5. Compensator pole at −2.5. Max phase frequency ω_m = √(z×p) = √(0.5×2.5) = √1.25 ≈ 1.118 rad/s.

questions.stem: A position control system with DC motor and gear train: (i) Draw the block diagram and find k1 (back-emf/torque constant) and k2 (tachometer constant) given motor constants and gear ratio N1/N2. (ii) Derive the closed-loop transfer function θL(s)/θR(s). (iii) Find the steady-state output θL(∞) for a ramp input of 1 rad/s.
common_errors: Incorrect gear reflection of inertia and damping (J_eff = a²J_L, B_eff = a²B_L)
Missing the tachometer feedback loop in block diagram
Forgetting to account for gear ratio when computing θL from θm
questions.solution_method: Reflect load inertia/damping through gear ratio a=N1/N2. Compute J_eff = a²J_L, B_eff = a²B_L. Set up motor equations. Block diagram has forward path Ks→KA→K1→1/(Js+B) and inner tachometer feedback K2. CL TF given as θL(s)/θR(s) = 120/(s²+4.2s+120). For ramp: apply FVT to find θL(∞) from sY(s)/s² as s→0; use s→0 limit of s·(120/(s²+4.2s+120))·(1/s²) → gives finite value m=0.087 unit.
key_formulas: θL(∞) for ramp = lim_{s→0} s · Y(s)

questions.stem: For the system G(s)H(s) = K(1+0.5s)(1+s) / [(1+10s)(s−1)], which has an unstable open-loop pole: (i) Sketch the polar plot and Nyquist contour. (ii) Determine stability using Nyquist criterion and find range of K for stability.
common_errors: Not accounting for open-loop RHP pole (P=1) in stability criterion Z=N+P
Incorrect direction of encirclement counting
Missing real-axis crossing calculation for Nyquist plot
questions.solution_method: P=1 (one open-loop RHP pole). Compute M(ω) and φ(ω). Find real-axis crossing: set imaginary part of GH(jω) to zero → ω=2.15, crossing at −0.167K. Nyquist: N must = −1 for Z=N+P=0 (stable). Encirclement of −1 occurs for appropriate K. Solution finds K>26 gives N=−1 → Z=0, stable. But also checks K<1/0.167=5.99 for no encirclement region. Range for stability: K>26.

questions.stem: For a hydraulic system with two tanks in series, derive the transfer function DQ0(s)/DQd(s) relating output flow to disturbance input, expressing it in terms of tank resistances R1, R2, capacitances C1, C2.
common_errors: Incorrect node balance equations for two-tank liquid level system
Forgetting to linearize (using Δ incremental variables)
questions.solution_method: Write incremental mass balance for each tank. Tank 1: DQ1−DQ2 = C1·d(DH1)/dt. Tank 2: DQ2−DQ0 = C2·d(DH2)/dt. Flow relations: DH1/R1 = DQ1, etc. Take Laplace and solve for DQ0/DQd. Result: DQ0/DQd = (1+R1C1s) / (R1R2C1C2s² + R1C1s + R2C2s + R1C2s + 1) simplified.

questions.stem: For a unity feedback system with open-loop TF C/R = K(s+5)/(s²+2s+ks+15k), design K using root locus such that: settling time ts < 800 ms and percent overshoot Mp < 43.2%. Also find steady-state error for unit ramp.
common_errors: Incorrect identification of open-loop poles and zeros for root locus
Not converting spec inequalities to ζ and ωn constraints correctly
Forgetting to verify both specs simultaneously
questions.solution_method: OL has 2 CL at −2 (origin shifted), 1 OL at −15. Breakaway at −1.035. Specs: ts<800ms → ζωn>5, Mp<43.2% → ζ>0.3. Using RL geometry: 2ζωn>0 and finding region in s-plane satisfying both. Solution finds 25.84 < K < 55.3. Steady-state error for ramp: ess = 1/Kv = (sum at s=0 limit), ess = 5/Kv = 0.167.

questions.stem: For G(s)H(s) = 4K / [s(s²+2s+4)]: (i) Sketch the Bode plot for K=1. (ii) Find gain margin and phase margin for K=1. (iii) Find K for 6 dB gain margin. (iv) Find K for just-unstable system.
common_errors: Not converting to time-constant form before sketching Bode plot
Missing complex pole corner frequency at ω=2
Incorrect phase at complex pole corner (not simply −90°)
questions.solution_method: Convert to time-constant form: K/[s(s²/4+s/2+1)]. Corner frequency at ω=2 (complex pole pair). Slope changes: −20 dB/dec for ω<2, then −60 dB/dec. Phase goes from −90° to −270°. For K=1: PM=56.31°, GM=6dB. System stable. For 6dB GM: K should be 1 (GM is already 6dB at K=1). For just-unstable: K must exceed value giving GM=0dB.
